title: Global Markets Brief — 18 May 2026
intro:
Date: 18 May 2026 • 06:29 CET
Risk-off sentiment dominates as global equities retreat amid renewed geopolitical tensions and elevated energy prices. Oil's sharp 2.12% rally signals supply concerns while precious metals face headwinds from dollar resilience. Asian session trading remains cautious with cross-asset volatility elevated and USD/JPY testing intervention territory above 158.80.

sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content:
Date: 18 May 2026 • 06:29 CET
Risk-off sentiment dominates as global equities retreat amid renewed geopolitical tensions and elevated energy prices. Oil's sharp 2.12% rally signals supply concerns while precious metals face headwinds from dollar resilience. Asian session trading remains cautious with cross-asset volatility elevated and USD/JPY testing intervention territory above 158.80.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
Ranking (strongest to weakest): GBP (+0.17%) > NZD (+0.10%) > USD/JPY (+0.10%) > EUR (+0.03%) > CHF (+0.03%) > CAD (-0.01%) > AUD (-0.14%)
Safe-haven flows favor USD and CHF while commodity currencies diverge on energy dynamics.

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1635 (+0.03%): Consolidating near 1.16 resistance. ECB dovish pivot concerns weigh on euro strength despite short-term bounce. Risk remains tilted lower.
USD/JPY 158.88 (+0.10%): Approaching critical 159.00 intervention zone. BoJ verbal warnings intensify but carry trade momentum persists. Topside capped by official resistance.
GBP/USD 1.3346 (+0.17%): Outperforming on UK inflation resilience. Cable benefits from relative BoE hawkishness but 1.34 represents key technical resistance.
AUD/USD 0.7143 (-0.14%): Weakest G10 performer despite energy rally. China growth concerns and RBA dovish tilt pressure Aussie below 0.72 support.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Heavy USD/JPY intervention hedging above 158.90 from Japanese corporates
• Euro zone real money reducing EUR exposure on ECB policy uncertainty
• Commodity currency positioning remains stretched long despite recent underperformance
• Cross-yen volatility elevated as carry trades face unwinding pressure

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: European markets lead declines: DAX -2.07% > FTSE -1.71% > CAC -1.60%. US futures point lower following yesterday's tech selloff led by Nasdaq -1.54%. Nikkei -0.95% outperforms on yen weakness supporting exporters. Energy sector strength contrasts with broad risk-off in financials and technology.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold $4540.50 (-0.47%): Correcting from record highs as dollar strength weighs. Key support at $4500 critical for bull trend continuation.
WTI Crude $103.16 (+2.12%): Breakout above $100 on supply disruption fears. Momentum targets $108 resistance with geopolitical premium expanding.
Silver $75.42 (-2.74%): Underperforming gold on industrial demand concerns. Support at $74 under pressure.
Natural Gas $3.039 (+2.67%): European supply fears drive energy complex higher alongside crude strength.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC $76689 (-0.94%): Bitcoin retreats from $77K resistance, testing $76K support. Risk-off flows pressure crypto alongside traditional assets. Key level at $75K.
ETH $2112 (-0.83%): Ethereum holds above $2100 psychological support. ETH/BTC ratio stabilizing at 0.0275. Regulatory clarity awaited.
Top mover: XRP $1.3809 (-1.43%) leads altcoin weakness. SOL $84.46 (-0.84%) consolidating. DOGE $0.1044 (-4.02%) most volatile on sentiment shift.

sections.heading: 🎯 Intraday Trade Ideas
sections.content:
📈 LONG — WTI crude on supply disruption momentum | USD/CHF on safe-haven demand | Energy sector rotation | GBP strength vs EUR cross
📉 SHORT — Silver on industrial weakness | AUD/USD China concerns | European bank stocks | DOGE volatility fade

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: Monitor: Asian PMI data releases, China credit data, US 10Y yield direction above 4.2%, ECB officials' commentary on policy outlook, Oil inventory reports.
